cross_scene_arcs.title: Enforcement of Media Discipline Against Simon Foster
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm escalates from monitoring Simon Foster's public statements to direct confrontation, sustained intimidation, and eventual dismissal, enforcing strict control over political messaging.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm begins monitoring Simon's radio interview as part of routine media surveillance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm declares Simon fired, fabricates authority from the Prime Minister, and demands a resignation letter.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Malcolm's authority to fire Simon remains questionable, and Simon's compliance is uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Consolidation and Collapse of Malcolm's Operational Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm asserts control over covert operations and intelligence efforts but is ultimately marginalized by U.S. officials and fails to deliver critical intelligence, leading to his public humiliation and loss of authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm initiates a two-pronged covert operation to suppress the Pwip Pip leak and influence media timing.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm is publicly called a 'useless piece of S**T' by Linton and fails to delay the UN vote or produce intelligence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Malcolm's ability to operate effectively is compromised, and his credibility is irreparably damaged.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Malcolm's Authoritarian Use of Language and Narrative Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm insists on controlling official narratives by denying spoken words, weaponizing misstatements like 'unforseeable,' and enforcing message discipline through intimidation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm denies that the word 'unforseeable' was spoken, asserting factual authority over perception.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He mocks Simon with a distorted version of the quote and enforces a single approved media line under threat.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The discrepancy between what was said and what is officially acknowledged remains unresolved, undermining trust in official narratives.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Deteriorating Relationship with Toby and Isolation of Power
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm uses Toby as a subordinate in confrontations with Simon, grows increasingly reliant on him, then threatens him into silence as his authority unravels.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby accompanies Malcolm into Simon's office, serving as a visible extension of Malcolm's authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm threatens Toby into silence after Simon's resignation, revealing his isolation and desperation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Toby's loyalty is now based on fear rather than trust, risking future disengagement or betrayal.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Suppression of Simon's Public Voice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's attempt to express a personal opinion on war leads to escalating suppression by Malcolm, culminating in a media blackout and public silencing within government meetings.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon defends policy position in media
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon is silenced and discredited in meetings, with no public platform
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon must either conform to the party line or remain excluded from public communication

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalating Power Struggle with Malcolm
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm systematically undermines Simon’s autonomy, using public reprimands, unannounced interventions, and coercive control, ending in Simon’s dismissal under false pretenses.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon becomes subject of Malcolm's anger after a media gaffe
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon is fired and threatened with media destruction if he resists
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon has no viable path to exit without being publicly destroyed

cross_scene_arcs.title: Simon's Moral and Emotional Unraveling
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon moves from moral hesitation about the war to active contingency planning and emotional breakdown, revealing deep anxiety and isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon avoids commitment while trying to preserve moral appearance
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon expresses suicidal ideation and emotional distress, feeling isolated
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lack of genuine emotional support from colleagues

cross_scene_arcs.title: Exploitation and Defiance in International Politics
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon is targeted for manipulation by U.S. officials due to his public stance, but later asserts independence by defying Karen Clark’s authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon is seen as a strategic asset to be manipulated
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon openly defies Karen Clark, asserting his autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the source of the leak remains unidentified, and conflict with Karen escalates

cross_scene_arcs.title: From outsider to political operator
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby transitions from a socially awkward newcomer into an active participant in high-level political operations, gaining access, responsibility, and inclusion in key decision-making circles.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: new advisor under scrutiny, associated with trivial domestic concerns
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: assigned to the Future Planning Committee, positioned within influential political networks
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether he can sustain credibility and competence in high-stakes roles

cross_scene_arcs.title: Subordination to Malcolm's control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby is progressively dominated and exploited by Malcolm, from initial mockery to being used as a political tool, ultimately placed under threat and discarded.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: subject to Malcolm's disdain and public ridicule
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: intimidated, on indefinite probation, and eventually replaced
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: fear of exposure and lack of agency under Malcolm's coercion

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting alliances within Team Simon
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby moves from being an uninvited presence to a trusted member of Simon's inner circle, aligning with Simon and Judy to manage team dynamics, then participating in Judy's exclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: sheepishly following Malcolm into a private meeting, without role
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: formally included in 'Team Simon', complicit in marginalizing Judy
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension from aligning with Simon against a senior colleague may undermine team cohesion

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic and ethical compromise with Liza
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby develops a personal relationship with Liza that blurs professional boundaries, leading to emotional exposure, moral hesitation, and failure to act on critical information.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: anticipating high-stakes political work with Liza on the Future Planning Committee
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally compromised, fails to leak Liza's paper after intimate involvement
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: no resolution on whether to act on politically sensitive information or define the relationship's boundaries

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral failure and isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby attempts to act on a moral imperative by sharing evidence that could stop a war but retreats under social pressure, leading to professional downfall and personal isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: attempting to leak Liza's paper to prevent war
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: discarded from his position, emotionally and professionally defeated
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether he will ever act decisively on ethical convictions in the face of pressure
